atoms.semantic_claim: Adds a direct dependency on `revm-context` so this crate can compile its async-context-aware ethereum EVM configuration against `revm_context` types.

destinations.evidence: The ethereum EVM crate now enables async-context support through its reth-evm dependency rather than a local direct revm-context dependency.
The lower local reth-evm crate now owns the feature forwarding to revm-context via revm/alloy instead of requiring ethereum/evm to import revm-context directly.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The direct `revm-context` dependency was not restored in reth-evm-ethereum because the current crate has no direct revm_context uses; the async-context behavior is restored through the feature graph owned by reth-evm and dependency crates.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run `cargo check -p reth-evm-ethereum --features async-context` in `/home/ritual/repos/ritual-reth-internal-v2.2.0-port`; expected failure is that the crate has no `async-context` feature and cannot compile downstream async-context integration from this crate.
atoms.notes: Feature graph check passed for reth-evm-ethereum --features async-context.

atoms.semantic_claim: Adds the `tracing` dependency required by downstream debug logging in this crate.

destinations.evidence: The ported block assembler no longer contains downstream `tracing::debug!` logging for oversized `extra_data`, so this crate does not currently need a direct `tracing` dependency.
The exact dependency line is absent from the destination manifest.

atoms.notes: This manifest atom tracks downstream-only logging that is not present in the v2.2.0 ported source. Guideline remediation: accepted as intentionally absent because The downstream `tracing` dependency only supported debug logging in `reth-evm-ethereum`; the ported block assembler has no corresponding logging call site.
atoms.intentional_absence_rationale: The downstream `tracing` dependency only supported debug logging in `reth-evm-ethereum`; the ported block assembler has no corresponding logging call site.

atoms.semantic_claim: Exposes an `async-context` feature from `reth-evm-ethereum` and forwards it into `reth-evm` so async-aware execution can be enabled transitively.

destinations.evidence: reth-evm-ethereum again exposes an async-context feature and forwards it into reth-evm.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The public async-context feature surface is restored on reth-evm-ethereum.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run `cargo check -p reth-evm-ethereum --features async-context` in `/home/ritual/repos/ritual-reth-internal-v2.2.0-port`; expected failure is an unknown feature on the crate, proving the downstream feature gate was not ported.
atoms.notes: Feature graph check passed for reth-evm-ethereum --features async-context.

summary: Atoms in this file are intentionally absent or mapped to active v2.2 behavior where applicable.
